name: Friends of KDSP
mission: Friends of KDSP supports the empowerment of the Down syndrome community in Pakistan by providing quality developmental services to individuals with Down syndrome and promoting and advocating for their inclusion in mainstream society.
theoryOfChange: Supports empowerment of the Down syndrome community in Pakistan by providing quality developmental services and promoting/advocating for inclusion in mainstream society; vision is one-stop access to quality affordable developmental services enabling independence and contribution to society.
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2022 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Early-stage organization. We show qualitative context while it builds a longer public track record.

keyConcerns.headline: Fundraising costs are 36% of total expenses
keyConcerns.detail: High fundraising spend reduces funds available for programs.

components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

alignment.rationale: Alignment 23/50: LOW Muslim donor fit, education global cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Seek external evaluation or track outcomes over at least 3 years.

score_details.score_summary: Friends of Kdsp shows moderate impact and moderate alignment (minor risk deductions applied). Evidence score may reflect limited reporting capacity, not program weakness.

all_citations.claim: The organization holds 28.5 months of working capital and has low data confidence.

The organization accepts general donations and sadaqah.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization receives a moderate score due to its specialized impact in a high-need region, balanced by a lower program expense ratio and limited external data verification.

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 23/50, the organization addresses an urgent global education and healthcare need but shows low specific fit for traditional Muslim donor priorities as it is strictly sadaqah-eligible [4]. The track record is established through its partnership with KDSP in Pakistan, though data confidence remains low at 0.3 [1][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The charity focuses on capacity building by facilitating surgeries and early childhood interventions, though its impact score of 27/50 reflects a moderate cost per beneficiary of approximately $125.79 [3]. While it maintains a healthy 28.5 months of working capital, the program expense ratio of 60.4% suggests room for improvement in direct service allocation [1][2].

baseline_narrative.headline: Friends of Kdsp empowers the Down syndrome community in Pakistan through comprehensive healthcare, early intervention, and educational integration.
strengths: High liquidity with 28.5 months of working capital available to sustain operations [1]
Facilitates critical medical procedures and mainstream school integration for beneficiaries [3]
Efficient fundraising operations with a cost of $0.23 per $1 raised [2]
baseline_narrative.summary: Friends of Kdsp is a U.S.-based nonprofit that supports the Karachi Down Syndrome Program (KDSP) to provide specialized services and advocacy for individuals with Down syndrome [1][3]. The organization reported a total revenue of $848,904 and operates with a program expense ratio of 60.4% [2].

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization receives a moderate score due to its specialized impact in a high-need region, balanced by a lower program expense ratio and limited external data verification.

areas_for_improvement.context: The <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 60.4%</cite> is lower than industry benchmarks for high-performing charities, suggesting a higher-than-average spend on administrative and fundraising costs.
The organization currently has <cite id="1">low data confidence</cite> due to limited third-party verification and the absence of a full Charity Navigator rating.

bbb_assessment.summary: This organization has not yet been evaluated by the BBB Wise Giving Alliance.

confidence.data_freshness: Financial data from 2023; Impact data from 2024
confidence.notes: Low confidence due to minimal third-party verification and small organizational scale.

credibility.explanation: Donors can verify the organization's existence through <cite id="2">IRS Form 990 filings</cite>, but credibility is currently limited by <cite id="1">low data confidence (0.3)</cite> and a <cite id="1">minimal board of only 3 members</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity should <cite id="1">expand its board of directors</cite> to include more independent members and seek a full rating from Charity Navigator.

impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="1">tracking outcomes over at least 3 years</cite> and seeking an external evaluation to verify the $125.79 per beneficiary cost-effectiveness.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support specialized disability advocacy and inclusive education in Pakistan through a highly liquid, growth-stage organization.
donor_motivations: Passionate about disability rights and inclusion
Want to support healthcare and education in Pakistan
Value organizations with high success rates in school integration
Prefer supporting niche causes that are often overlooked by large humanitarian agencies
giving_considerations: Donations are treated as sadaqah, not zakat
The organization has a lower program expense ratio than some larger peers
High cash reserves mean your donation contributes to long-term stability
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those who prioritize charities with program expense ratios above 80%.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects <cite id="3">documented outcomes</cite> like surgery counts and integration rates, but lacks <cite id="1">independent third-party impact evaluations</cite>.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The organization uses a <cite id="3">clear theory of change</cite> focusing on early intervention and healthcare to move individuals from dependence to <cite id="3">mainstream educational and social integration</cite>.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that specialized interventions like therapy and inclusive education actually result in long-term social integration for beneficiaries.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="1">revenue of $655,185</cite> and a large underserved population in Pakistan, the organization has significant capacity to scale its <cite id="3">healthcare and education programs</cite>.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="3">80% mainstream school integration rate</cite> specifically for the Down syndrome community in Pakistan.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains <cite id="1">28.5 months of working capital</cite> in working capital, ensuring long-term sustainability and the ability to weather funding fluctuations.
Friends of Kdsp achieves an <cite id="3">80% mainstream school integration rate</cite>, demonstrating the effectiveness of their early intervention and inclusive education programs.
The charity has successfully <cite id="3">facilitated 400 surgeries and medical procedures</cite>, addressing the specific healthcare vulnerabilities of the Down syndrome community.

strengths_deep_dive: Friends of Kdsp demonstrates a strong commitment to measurable outcomes in specialized care. Their intervention model is not merely about support but about systemic integration, as evidenced by their <cite id="3">Skills Development and Inclusive Education</cite> programs. By focusing on <cite id="3">early childhood intervention</cite>, they prepare beneficiaries for mainstream society, which is reflected in their high integration metrics. Financially, the organization is lean, reporting <cite id="2">total liabilities of only $528</cite> against <cite id="2">total assets of $461,455</cite>, indicating a very low debt profile and high net equity.
rich_narrative.summary: Friends of Kdsp is a U.S.-based nonprofit dedicated to supporting the Karachi Down Syndrome Program (KDSP) in Pakistan, focusing on <cite id="3">comprehensive healthcare, early intervention, and educational integration</cite> for individuals with Down syndrome. The organization serves as a critical funding bridge, reporting <cite id="2">$655,185 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year. Their mission centers on creating an inclusive society where individuals with Down syndrome are valued and provided with the tools to lead fulfilling lives. This is achieved through a multi-pronged approach that includes <cite id="3">Sehat Healthcare, Early Childhood Intervention, and Skills Development</cite>. A significant portion of their impact is delivered through <cite id="3">facilitating surgeries and medical procedures</cite>, with recent reports indicating <cite id="3">400 such procedures facilitated</cite> for beneficiaries. Furthermore, the organization emphasizes long-term self-sufficiency by maintaining an <cite id="3">80% mainstream school integration rate</cite>, ensuring that children with Down syndrome can transition into standard educational environments. Financially, the charity maintains a highly liquid position with <cite id="1">28.5 months of working capital</cite> in reserve, providing a substantial safety net for its international operations. While the organization operates with <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.23 per $1 raised</cite>, it remains a niche entity within the global humanitarian space, focusing specifically on disability advocacy and support within the Pakistani context.
